danger_signals.visual_form: Designated target enemy displayed before stage begins
danger_signals.meaning: Player must identify and destroy specific threat to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Armored insect-like creatures integrated into enemy bodies
danger_signals.meaning: World state: civilization corrupted by parasitic enhancement; enemies are enhanced super-criminals

danger_signals.visual_form: Interdimensional door opening with time limit (10 seconds)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat: failure to enter portal results in body destruction/loss of life

danger_signals.visual_form: Player projectile power level decreases visually upon damage
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state: Ken's cybernetic systems weakening

danger_signals.visual_form: Spread across planets; turns hosts into 'violent, mindless supermen or superaliens'
danger_signals.meaning: World crisis: pandemic of enhancement creating uncontrollable super-beings

safety_signals.visual_form: Item containers scattered throughout stages that grant projectile level increases
safety_signals.meaning: Progression toward safety: collecting two capsules increases range and strength

safety_signals.visual_form: Special power-up equipment allowing damage during flip jumps
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive capability unlock; explicitly mentioned as provided by antagonist to aid progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Interdimensional door becomes accessible after destroying targets
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage to next stage; objective completion

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kevin is Galaxy Police cyborg; enemies are 'Parasites' (super-criminals with parasitic enhancements)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Multiple frontier planets serve as Parasite strongholds; each planet reflects Parasite infestation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Armored insect-like parasitic organisms visibly integrated into host bodies; appear as 'superaliens' or enhanced super-beings
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Planets corrupted by Cyboplasm spread; environmental degradation reflects infection levels

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Final boss: revealed as architect of Parasites; controls army of enhanced super-criminals
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final confrontation on planet where antagonist maintains control; likely heavily fortified

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ken as martial arts champion (referenced in backstory, not shown in-game)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Retired from fighting; civilian scientist role

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ken transformed with cybernetic implants and interdimensional transporter
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ken forced into combat role; pursuing killer across planets

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Designated target enemies with parasitic enhancements visible on-stage; stage design reflects planet type (sand planets, etc.)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively hostile; player must destroy to gather energy and open portal

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ken experiences 'strange pain in body'; internal Cyboplasm implant unknown to player until revelation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ken continues pursuit despite internal threat; physical discomfort increases as story progresses

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ken returns to Earth; Cyboplasm threat becomes pandemic requiring containment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ken shifts from revenge to crisis management

environmental_storytelling.example: Sand planet 'Dagoba' referenced; each frontier world visited reflects contamination by antagonist's travel path
Worlds explicitly become 'quickly infested' after killer's visit; infestation visible through enemy compositions and stage design
Antagonist reveals flip shield capsule location as 'help,' visually demonstrating antagonist's knowledge and control over Ken's progression
Interdimensional doors and time limits reflect the technological nature of frontier worlds and stakes of failure
Stage-by-stage enemy composition reflects degree of parasitic infection in each region

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Continues forward despite internal Cyboplasm implant causing pain; prioritizes mission over personal safety
Taunts Ken with warnings; provides strategic information (flip shield location) to extend Ken's chase; maintains psychological pressure
Must be destroyed on sight; no negotiation possible; represent hazardous world state
Deploy Kevin via communication; provide mission briefing; establish rules (10-second portal window = life-or-death mechanism)

notes: This game communicates world-state primarily through: (1) Cyboplasm as a visual/conceptual pandemic spreading across frontier planets, (2) Enemy parasite designs showing civilization corruption, (3) Time-limited portal mechanics creating mechanical world danger, (4) Two localization versions create different factional contexts: Ken as retired fighter (US) vs. Kevin as Galaxy Police enforcer (JP). The game's core manifestation system uses stage-by-stage target destruction as both mechanical progression and visual proof of infestation levels. Ken's internal corruption (Cyboplasm implant) represents hidden threat manifestation—danger the player cannot see but character experiences through dialogue. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on implied planetary infestation rather than explicit visual degradation in source material.
